hawkish_phrases: some modest tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period is likely to be necessary
spare capacity has been eroded over the past couple of quarters
the economy is projected to have a margin of excess demand for a period
CPI inflation is projected to rise temporarily in the near term, to 4% in 2021 Q4
continuing with asset purchases when CPI inflation was 4% and the output gap was closed...might cause medium-term inflation expectations to drift higher
dovish_phrases: voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.1%
The Committee would not put undue weight on capacity pressures that are frictional in nature and likely to be temporary
current elevated global and domestic cost pressures will prove transitory
in the medium term, conditioned on the market path for interest rates, inflation is projected to fall back to close to the 2% target
downside risks to GDP were judged to be skewed to the downside of the central forecast
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to hold Bank Rate at 0.1% and 7-1 to continue asset purchases (Saunders dissenting hawkishly), but signaled that some modest tightening over the forecast period is likely to be necessary, with inflation projected to reach 4% and spare capacity eroded.
